text:
Market today: SPY/QQQ holding up on surface but under the hood it's a weak jobs print (57K payrolls, unemployment 4.2%) doing the real driving. Soft labor = rate-cut hopes keeping indices bid, but it also signals slowing growth - a mixed signal for growth-heavy tech and chips specifically.

Your holdings: CRWD is the emergency - you're down 72.91% and your stop is 590 on a $194 stock, meaning that stop is useless and you have no protection. SOXL is down 30.84% - it's a 3x leveraged ETF that decays daily, and on margin that drawdown compounds fast. SPAL is a new leveraged ETF already down 15.11% with a stop of 21.38 basically at current price (21.35) - it should be triggering or already stopped.

Watch: Whether SPAL's stop (21.38) triggers today given it's trading right at it. Also watch SMH 580 - it's your semiconductor benchmark and scanner shows support around 586.

Bottom line: Fix your broken CRWD stop and get clear on SOXL size before anything else - margin makes these holes get very deep, very fast.
plays: CRWD - your stop of 590 is meaningless at $194; decide today whether you're holding intentionally with no stop or cutting the loss - leaving it unprotected on margin is the real risk here.
SPAL stop is 21.38 and price is 21.35 - this is basically triggered; if it hasn't closed you out yet, check it now and don't let a leveraged ETF run past its stop on margin.
SMH - you're already long, scanner shows ACCUMULATE with support at 586.38-586.96, stop 580.77; if it holds that zone today, no action needed, but a break of 580 should have you trimming.
